title: microliabum eremophilum
intro: Microliabum eremophilum, also known as Poverty Weed, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Consuming Poverty Weed has been reported to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating cold and flu symptoms, and even helping to manage certain types of skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
